architecture.summary: A neural-symbolic system that stores and retrieves knowledge from a symbolic knowledge base (KG) by learning a mapping between entity/relation embeddings and neural activations. The system uses a trainable bridge (learned KV projections or soft prompts) to inject retrieved symbolic knowledge into a language model's hidden states, enabling the model to condition generation on external symbolic facts without modifying the base model weights.
trained_components: bridge projector mapping symbolic representations to model activations

biblio.title: Adaptable and Interpretable Neural MemoryOver Symbolic Knowledge

claims.statement: The model learns an interpretable and adaptable neural memory over symbolic knowledge, enabling retrieval and integration of facts from a knowledge base into neural generation
The approach allows adaptation to new knowledge without retraining the base language model, by learning new entity and relation embeddings and updating the retrieval mechanism
